headline: Iran Launches Massive Missile Barrage Across All Israel
summary: Iran fired dozens of ballistic missiles at Israel targeting cities from the northern Galilee through Tel Aviv to Jerusalem, triggering nationwide Red Alert sirens. A missile struck Beit Shemesh near Jerusalem, killing 9 civilians in a synagogue shelter and injuring over 20. A separate missile struck a building in Tel Aviv, killing 1 and injuring dozens. This is part of Iran's retaliatory wave following the joint US-Israeli military operation launched February 28.

secondary_markets.name: RTX Corporation (Raytheon)
secondary_markets.reason: Co-manufacturer of Iron Dome interceptors with Rafael; stock already up 4.7% with continued demand for missile-defense systems as Iran sustains barrages.

secondary_markets.name: Northrop Grumman
secondary_markets.reason: Stealth-bomber and missile-defense technologies in high demand; shares surged 6% on initial strike day and war premium persists.

secondary_markets.name: Lockheed Martin
secondary_markets.reason: Prime contractor for F-35s actively used in the operation and THAAD missile defense; stock climbed 3.3% with further upside expected.

secondary_markets.name: Elbit Systems
secondary_markets.reason: Israel's largest publicly-traded defense company providing precision-guided munitions, UAVs, and electronic warfare systems under record wartime demand.

secondary_markets.name: El Al Israel Airlines
secondary_markets.reason: Israeli airspace closed, Ben Gurion Airport shut down; antitrust fine for price gouging and fleet grounded with only limited evacuation flights permitted.

trade_position: Long RTX calls (3-month expiry): as the sole Western Iron Dome interceptor co-manufacturer, every sustained Iranian barrage depletes Israeli Tamir missile stocks and accelerates the $1.25B replenishment contract signed in November 2025. This is a recurring revenue catalyst that compounds with each attack wave.

messages.text_en: ONCE AGAIN: Iran is attacking civilians across Israel
